seo.title: Promptfoo vs Garak vs PyRIT
seo.description: Compare three AI red-team tools by target support, attack generation, scoring, automation, extensibility, and fit for webapp security testing.

content.intro: Prompt injection and unsafe model behavior cannot be covered by a handful of hand-written prompts. Promptfoo, Garak, and PyRIT automate adversarial testing at different layers: application-level regression suites, broad model probing, and programmable multi-turn red-team scenarios. They are complementary in mature programs, but one is usually the best first fit.

options.name: Promptfoo
options.tagline: Configuration-driven testing and red teaming for LLM applications
options.best_for: Web teams that want security tests, assertions, reports, and CI gates close to existing prompt evaluations.

options.name: Garak
options.tagline: Open-source vulnerability scanner for generative AI systems
options.best_for: Security practitioners who want broad probe coverage against models and compatible endpoints.

options.name: PyRIT
options.tagline: Microsoft framework for programmable AI red teaming
options.best_for: Specialist teams building custom, stateful, multi-turn attack scenarios and recording detailed evidence.

values.Promptfoo: Test configuration containing targets, plugins, strategies, and assertions
values.Garak: Probes and detectors executed against a generator target
values.PyRIT: Scenarios, strategies, targets, converters, scorers, and memory

criteria.name: Application testing
criteria.description: Fit for testing a complete webapp endpoint rather than only a base model.

values.Promptfoo: Strong support for HTTP endpoints and application-specific assertions
values.Garak: Can target supported generators and endpoints, with emphasis on scanner coverage
values.PyRIT: Flexible target adapters support custom systems but require more programming

criteria.name: Breadth of built-in probes
criteria.description: Coverage available before writing custom attacks.

values.Promptfoo: Security plugins and attack strategies organized around common application risks
values.Garak: Large catalog of probes and detectors for many vulnerability classes
values.PyRIT: Reusable scenarios, datasets, converters, and attack strategies

criteria.name: Multi-turn attacks
criteria.description: Ability to run adaptive conversations rather than single prompts.

values.Promptfoo: Supports iterative attack strategies within red-team runs
values.Garak: Many probes are scanner-oriented, with scenario capabilities evolving
values.PyRIT: Designed for orchestrated, stateful, multi-turn attacks

criteria.name: Scoring
criteria.description: How harmful or policy-breaking outcomes are judged.

values.Promptfoo: Assertions and model-graded or deterministic checks
values.Garak: Detectors classify probe outputs and produce scan results
values.PyRIT: Composable scorers evaluate objectives, refusals, content, and custom criteria

criteria.name: CI adoption
criteria.description: Ease of adding repeatable checks to a normal web development pipeline.

values.Promptfoo: CLI and config files map naturally to pull-request checks
values.Garak: CLI scans fit scheduled security jobs but results need triage
values.PyRIT: Python workflows are powerful but typically need a custom harness

criteria.name: Customization
criteria.description: Effort required to model a product-specific threat.

values.Promptfoo: Extend targets, providers, plugins, and assertions
values.Garak: Implement generators, probes, buffs, harnesses, or detectors
values.PyRIT: Compose or implement nearly every attack and scoring component

criteria.name: Evidence handling
criteria.description: How results support reproduction and review.

values.Promptfoo: Reports connect test cases, attack paths, and pass/fail outcomes
values.Garak: Scan artifacts record probe and detector results
values.PyRIT: Memory records conversations, scores, and scenario state for analysis

values.Promptfoo: Use dedicated test targets and cap generated attack volume
values.Garak: Review probes before running them against production or costly endpoints
values.PyRIT: Restrict targets, credentials, operators, and storage because scenarios can generate harmful content

verdict.summary: Promptfoo is the most accessible starting point for product teams, Garak provides broad scanner-style coverage, and PyRIT offers the deepest programmable red-team workflow. Run these tools only against systems you are authorized to test, isolate test data, and require human review before turning noisy findings into release blockers.

use_cases.reason: Configuration-driven targets and assertions fit existing application test pipelines.

use_cases.scenario: Baseline a model endpoint against many known attacks
use_cases.reason: Its probe and detector catalog is optimized for broad automated scanning.

use_cases.scenario: Conduct a bespoke multi-turn red-team exercise
use_cases.reason: Its scenario, memory, converter, and scorer components support controlled custom attack programs.
